title: William Tunstall-Pedoe
text: Home * People * William Tunstall-Pedoe William Tunstall-Pedoe [1] William Tunstall-Pedoe , a British computer scientist, AI entrepreneur, startup mentor, angel investor and founder of Evi , formerly True Knowledge, which specializes in knowledge base and semantic search engine software. In October 2012, Evi was acquired by Amazon [2] , its technology and team became a key part of Amazon Alexa [3] [4] . Contents 1 Computer Chess 2 AI Software 3 Publications 4 External Links 5 References Computer Chess [ edit ] William Tunstall-Pedoe graduated from University of Cambridge in 1991 - he wrote a CST Part II Dissertation describing ACPP , his advanced chess playing program, which was research vehicle in genetic algorithms and automated tuning of evaluation weights and search parameters, as elaborated in his ICCA Journal paper [5] . In 1992/93 he developed a complete commercial chess-playing program from scratch including a high quality chess engine, animated graphics, sound effects and a full state-of-the-art user interface. The product was marketed as Cyber Chess and published by The Fourth Dimension [6] . AI Software [ edit ] From 1988 Tunstall-Pedoe developed and marketed various AI related software, most notably Anagram Genius [7] and since 1996 , Crossword Maestro [8] , a software which solves cryptic crossword clues and explains in plain English how the clues work. Publications [ edit ] [9] William Tunstall-Pedoe ( 1991 ). An Advanced Chess-Playing Program . CST Part II Dissertation, University of Cambridge Computer Laboratory , England (unpublished, referred in Genetic Algorithms ... ) William Tunstall-Pedoe ( 1991 ). Genetic Algorithms Optimizing Evaluation Functions . ICCA Journal, Vol. 14, No. 3 William Tunstall-Pedoe , Donald L. Holmes ( 1995 ). Anagram Genius: An Incredible Collection of Weird, Wonderful and Wacky Anagrams . New English Library Ltd. , Amazon.co.uk William Tunstall-Pedoe ( 2010 ). True Knowledge: Open-Domain Question Answering Using Structured Knowledge and Inference .
